reasoning: Based on an exhaustive search of international carbon pricing developments (via ICAP, OECD, and news), all primary candidate countries for new carbon pricing instruments (such as Brazil, Turkey, India, Vietnam, Thailand, Malaysia, and the Philippines) have already announced their new systems or passed enabling legislation prior to the strict cutoff date of November 4, 2025. While some of these countries had target notifications or detailed framework rollouts in late 2025 or early 2026, the systems themselves were announced beforehand, which disqualifies them according to the resolution criteria ("If a system was announced before 4 November 2025, it will not count..."). With only 3.5 months remaining in the forecast period (April 12, 2026, to July 31, 2026) and no new, previously unannounced countries showing sudden legislative momentum, the chance of a qualifying announcement materializing is low. The remaining probability covers unexpected announcements from emerging economies currently studying carbon pricing.
